title: Green Dragon Farmers Market
location: Green Dragon Farmers Market, 955 North State Street, Ephrata, 17522, United States
description:
Green Dragon Farmer’s Market: A True Lancaster County Experience!
Millions of shoppers have visited this treasured Lancaster County Amish market since 1932! 
Enjoy a beautiful Friday morning at Lancaster’s favorite local farmer’s market! The Green Dragon Farmers Market & Auction sits humbly on a spacious 60-acre site in the heart of Amish Country. Since its inception in 1932, we’ve grown to become one of the largest flea markets on the East Coast.

title: 2026 Lancaster Cool Schools Mobile Polar Plunge
description:
Join us for the Lancaster Cool Schools Polar Plunge on Friday, March 13 at JP McCaskey High School to experience the magic of Plunge and support Special Olympics Pennsylvania athletes by jumping into our mobile plunge unit – Bear Force One! 
Every dollar raised helps support vital programming for individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities, ensuring quality training, competition, and leadership opportunities for students at new and exsisting Special Olympics PA Unified Champion Schools! 
Plungers commit to “Freezin’ for a Reason” and raise a minimum of $35 to dip a toe, wade, or jump into icy waters. 
So, create your team and join thousands of plungers around the state in making a splash to support our athletes

description: A community-wide conspiracy designed to bring laughter to Lancaster Join us for a casual night out in downtown Lancaster City that will have you rolling in the aisles…well, aisle! GO BULLDOGS It’s tradition around these parts that whenever you hear someone say “Go Bulldogs!”, you have to reply “GO BULLDOGS!” GBD is Lancaster Improv Players longest running team; each show is a randomized selection of players from our entire community of students, coaches, and performers. that means once you finish an improv course at LIP, you can be a Bulldog, too! title: Shakedown Citi
location: Mickey’s Black Box, 101 Rock Lititz Boulevard, Lititz, PA, 17543, United States
description:
Friday March 13th, 2026
Doors | 7:00 PM
Show | 8:00 PM 
General Admission Standing Room | $22.40 + Fees
Reserved Riser Seating | $33.65 + Fees
Reserved Balcony & Mezzanine Seating | $33.65 + Fees 
+$5 on All Day of Show Purchases
This event is all ages – 21+ With Valid ID to Drink
Come hungry! Snacks and light bites will be available at the canteen.
—————————————————
Presented by Big Fan Presents
—————————————————
Shakedown Citi is a fresh new force on the jam band scene, born in the heart of a Grateful Dead parking lot at Citi Field in 2023.  When the band’s members crossed paths on Shakedown Street, something clicked – and the music hasn’t stopped since. 
Rather than imitating the Dead, Shakedown Citi channels their spirit – creating spontaneous, soulful performances that feel alive, adventurous and totally in the moment. Each set is a journey, full of unexpected twists, deep grooves and musical conversation that pulls even the most skeptical listener into the vibe.  Every Shakedown Citi show begins long before the first note.  Each setlist is thoughtfully and meticulously crafted from the Dead repertoire, affiliated catalogs and beyond, balancing deep cuts and beloved anthems, rarities and reprises.  No two nights are ever the same – each performance stands as a work of art on its own, alive in its moment, while also fitting into the larger patchwork of the repertoire.   
It’s not just a tribute – it’s the Dead, reborn. 
Check out Shakedown Citi here!
Website | Instagram | Facebook

title: HAMMER CREEK- LIVE BLUEGRASS AT ZOETROPOLIS
location: The Zoetropolis Theater, 112 N Water Street, Lancaster, Pennsylvania, 17602, United States
description: We invite you to join us at the fantastic Zoetropolis Theater in beautiful Lancaster City for a cozy evening of tradition and roots done our way. Hammer Creek Bluegrass, Rooted in Lancaster, PA is a bluegrass band dedicated to preserving the rich traditions of the genre while celebrating its evolution. Our music takes you back to the roots of bluegrass, honoring legendary pioneers like Flatt & Scruggs, The Stanley Brothers, and Bill Monroe. But we don’t stop there—we also pay tribute to the transitional era, drawing inspiration from artists like Del McCoury and J.D. Crowe, who helped shape the sound of modern bluegrass. Whether you’re a die-hard bluegrass fan or simply looking for a night of great live music, Hammer Creek delivers the timeless songs of simpler days. With driving banjo, heartfelt harmonies, and that unmistakable high lonesome sound, we bring bluegrass to life—one song at a time. Come join us for an evening of tradition, passion, and pickin’ done right.

title: Liz Fulmer & Matt Goss
location: The Ware Center – Millersville University, 42 N. Prince Street, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description: Treat yourself to a night under the stars featuring the best of local music! Lancaster County has a rich faith-based tradition. The music—and the musicians—born out of that deep history can resonate powerfully across genres. Join us for an evening of good-for-the-soul original music with two of Lancaster’s most beloved and talented singer/songwriters who have roots in music ministry. Taking place in the stunning glass pyramid-topped Atrium of the Ware Center, the “Lancaster Loves” series features the best in local music across a variety of genres in a lively cabaret setting.

title: Celtic Woman at American Music Theatre
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Highway East, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Grammy nominated global music sensation Celtic Woman is returning to AMT!  Celtic Woman celebrates the rich musical and cultural heritage of Ireland, taking audiences on a journey of story and song from across the Emerald Isle.  Embrace an evening of angelic voices, breath-taking harmonies, and instrumental virtuosity, transporting you from your theatre seat to the lush landscapes of Ireland.  Celtic Woman’s fresh fusion of traditional Irish music and contemporary songcraft celebrates Ireland’s ancient heritage while reflecting the vibrant spirit of modern Ireland.  With Irish dancers, bagpipers, and an array of traditional Irish instruments – including the bodhran, tin whistle, and uilleann pipes – this dynamic and multi-talented group has captivated audiences all around the world.  Join us for this very special show and experience the beauty of Ireland brought to life by Celtic Woman.

title: Evening Compline
location: Saint James Episcopal Church, 103 North Duke Street, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Evening Compline
via Zoom
Click here to join Compline. (This link can be used for every evening Compline on Zoom.) 

The short service of Compline is based on the closing time of prayer in the Daily Office, or monastic cycle of prayer. It is observed in monasteries around 8 or 9 PM when darkness has fallen and bedtime is near. 

The thoughts that inspire the prayers during Compline are the need for divine forgiveness and protection against the powers of darkness. Prayers for peace and rest are offered as night falls. 

People of all faiths and beliefs are invited to this contemplative service. The service lasts about 20 minutes.

title: Joshua at Sight & Sound Theatre
location: Sight & Sound Theatre, 300 Hartman Bridge Road, Strasburg, PA, 17579, United States
description:
Be strong and courageous
From the banks of the Jordan River to the towering walls of Jericho, JOSHUA is a miraculous adventure for the whole family. Journey alongside the people of Israel as they navigate their new-found freedom in an unforgiving wilderness. When Joshua is called to replace Moses as their faithful leader, tensions mount in the midst of dwindling supplies and approaching armies. With one last fortified city standing between the Israelites and the Promised Land, unexpected help emerges from behind enemy lines, and Joshua’s trust in God’s plan is put to the ultimate test. 
Experience one of the most redemptive stories of the Bible as it comes to life in this brand-new original stage production from Sight & Sound Theatres.

title: St. Patrick's Day at Annie Bailey's Irish Public House
location: Annie Bailey’s Irish Pub & Restaurant, 28-30 East King Street, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
St. Patrick’s Day at Annie Bailey’s isn’t just a day—it’s a five-day celebration running March 13th through March 17th. As one of the only true Irish pubs in town, this is what we do best. We’ll be pouring all your Irish favorites—Guinness, Harp Lager, Smithwick’s, and Magners—alongside a special food menu packed with hearty Irish staples. 



Expect late-night entertainment all weekend long, plus a special appearance by Irish dancers bringing authentic energy and tradition to the pub. On St. Patrick’s Day itself, we’re opening early so you can celebrate from the first pint to the last call. Gather your crew, wear your green, and stop in to experience St. Patrick’s the way it’s meant to be—loud, lively, and unmistakably Irish. 



Sláinte! 🍻

title: Lancaster Breakfast and History Walking Tour
location: Unique Lancaster, 23 N Walnut Street, Lancaster, 17603, United States
description:
Searching for a walking history tour in Lancaster? You’re in for a flavorful experience—literally! 


Introducing the Lancaster History & Breakfast Walking Tour—a dynamic two-hour adventure that blends the rich heritage of one of America’s oldest inland cities with a delicious morning meal. Explore Lancaster’s vibrant streets with a professionally trained local guide and journey through time from the early 1700s through the transformative decades of the 20th century. 


Brought to you by Unique Lancaster Experiences, this guided walk kicks off with your choice of hot or iced coffee or tea to energize your morning. Along the way, you’ll hear engaging stories about Lancaster’s evolution as a bustling Market City and its transition from trolleys to automobiles after WWI. 


But this isn’t your standard history tour. You’ll dive into interactive discussions and uncover captivating insights about colonial life, architectural landmarks, and the impact of prohibition on the region. 


As you explore, you’ll stop at five handpicked local spots to enjoy a curated tasting menu featuring hearty breakfast sandwiches, fresh fruits, sweet pastries, and other morning favorites. 


Please note: This tour offers vegetarian-friendly options only. If you have other dietary needs, we recommend our International Food Tour for a better fit. 


Whether you’re passionate about history, love discovering new flavors, or just want a memorable way to see the city, the Lancaster History & Breakfast Tour is a great choice for solo explorers, couples, and groups alike.

title: Philly Bike Expo via the Schuylkill River Trail - 30/35 F - RSVP Required
description:
Lancaster Bicycle Club (LBC) Meetup
Join our annual casual ride to the Philly Bike Expo at the Philadelphia Convention Center. We will ride mostly on the paved Schuylkill River Trail and turn off onto Race Street riding city streets to our destination. We will be using the Manayunk towpath for a couple of miles which is not paved. Road bikes can be used on this section, but it could be a little rough.

This ride is cross-posted with the Wilmington Biking Rails to Trails Group. Option, for those who wish, coming back ride the new extension South on the trail.

Tickets are $25 at the door or they can be purchased prior to the date for $20 here: https://phillybikeexpo.com/events/2026-philly-bike-expo

Other expo information can be found here: https://phillybikeexpo.com

We park our bikes inside the Convention Center using their complimentary valet bike parking. After we visit the exhibits, we will walk across the street to the Reading Terminal Market for lunch before returning to Conshohocken.

Rain date is the next day, Sunday, March 15th.

Helmets are mandatory. No ear buds. Please bring water and a basic repair kit (spare tube, tire levers, pump/CO2 inflator).

To eliminate passing around a pen and paper waiver, we require LBC Club membership and Meetup RSVP below to join this ride.

Being on Meetup does not mean you are a club member. [Join here](https://www.lancasterbikeclub.net/content.aspx?page_id=60&club_id=257148).

Your RSVP is your agreement to abide by the club's [Code of Conduct](https://lancbikeclub.clubexpress.com/content.aspx?page_id=22&club_id=257148&module_id=323984) ,
[Rules of the Road](https://lancbikeclub.clubexpress.com/content.aspx?page_id=22&club_id=257148&module_id=325159) , and [Ride Waiver](https://lancasterbikeclub.net/docs.ashx?id=454318)

title: Brewery Tours: Lancaster City Walk-a-Bout
location: PA
description:
Discover Lancaster City One Sip at a Time! 



Step into the heart of craft beer country with Brewery Tours’ Lancaster City Walk-a-Bout! 



Join our fun and knowledgeable Beer Guides as we stroll through beautiful downtown Lancaster City, visiting three of the city’s best craft breweries along the way. Guests will enjoy guided tastings of twelve incredible local beers while hearing behind-the-scenes brewery stories and discovering the history, culture, and character that makes Lancaster’s craft beer scene so special. 



What to Expect: 



– Visit three Lancaster City craft breweries 



– Guided tastings of a curated selection of locally brewed beers 



– Entertaining beer history and brewery insights 



– A relaxed walking experience through vibrant downtown Lancaster 



Whether you’re a craft beer enthusiast or just looking for a fun way to explore the city, the Lancaster City Walk-a-Bout is the perfect blend of beer, history, and adventure. 



Grab your friends, lace up your walking shoes, and let’s explore Lancaster—one great beer at a time. 



Book your spot today with Brewery Tours! 



This craft beer experience runs most Saturdays throughout the year. Advanced booking is required at www.UltimateCraftBeerExperience.com.  



This is a 21+ experience only.

—————————————————
Screaming Orphans are a chart-topping all-sister folk and pop band from Ireland. They are known worldwide for their award-winning sound combining original pop songs with a unique take on traditional Irish music. The band has always defied being pigeonholed, having masterfully developed their own unique fusion of folk and pop. The sisters’ trademark spine-tingling four-part harmony and mastery of their instruments, combined with their highly energized performances, take audiences of all ages on an exhilarating and emotional musical journey. 
Screaming Orphans’ pitch perfect harmonies have led them to many collaborations on recordings and stage with artists such as Sinéad O’Connor, Peter Gabriel, Joni Mitchell, Baaba Maal and Afro Celt Sound System. Their albums have topped Billboard, iTunes and World Music charts. 
Check out the Screaming Orphans here!

title: Evening Compline
location: Saint James Episcopal Church, 103 North Duke Street, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Evening Compline
via Zoom
Click here to join Compline. (This link can be used for every evening Compline on Zoom.) 

The short service of Compline is based on the closing time of prayer in the Daily Office, or monastic cycle of prayer. It is observed in monasteries around 8 or 9 PM when darkness has fallen and bedtime is near. 

The thoughts that inspire the prayers during Compline are the need for divine forgiveness and protection against the powers of darkness. Prayers for peace and rest are offered as night falls. 

People of all faiths and beliefs are invited to this contemplative service. The service lasts about 20 minutes.

title: St. Patrick's Day at Annie Bailey's Irish Public House
location: Annie Bailey’s Irish Pub & Restaurant, 28-30 East King Street, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
St. Patrick’s Day at Annie Bailey’s isn’t just a day—it’s a five-day celebration running March 13th through March 17th. As one of the only true Irish pubs in town, this is what we do best. We’ll be pouring all your Irish favorites—Guinness, Harp Lager, Smithwick’s, and Magners—alongside a special food menu packed with hearty Irish staples. 



Expect late-night entertainment all weekend long, plus a special appearance by Irish dancers bringing authentic energy and tradition to the pub. On St. Patrick’s Day itself, we’re opening early so you can celebrate from the first pint to the last call. Gather your crew, wear your green, and stop in to experience St. Patrick’s the way it’s meant to be—loud, lively, and unmistakably Irish.

title: Longford Park and Reynold's Dog Park- route along canal
description:
Walking Tails
This week we will walk along the canal. The trail in this direction is dirt and gravel. We will go out about 1.5 miles and then come back the way we came, for a total of 3 miles. CLICK DIRECTLY ON THE MAP IF YOU THINK YOU ARE LOST. IT WILL BRING YOU RIGHT TO US WHERE WE MEET.

We will be meeting near the dog park, come early and let your dog play off leash in the dog park.

Start Time: We will start hiking promptly at the scheduled time.

Duration of the walk – depends on the people and dogs in the group. We frequently end up walking in smaller packs based on the comfort level of the participants. In the past this walk takes about 1 hour or so.

Please remember pooh bags and that your dog must be on a leash that is no more than 10 feet long.

Facilities: There are bathrooms at the dog park

NOTICE: By attending this event you are agreeing that YOU ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR YOUR OWN SAFETY AT ALL TIMES. The hike leader and other members cannot be held responsible for any damages, accidents, or liabilities incurred while hiking with us.

title: Spring Equinox Yoga
location: Gallery Grow Plant Bar, 320 N Queen St., Lancaster, Pennsylvania, 17603, United States
description:
Welcome spring with a gentle, beginner-friendly yoga class focused on breath, movement, and the calming presence of plants. 



Join yoga teacher Maggie Mowery for an hour-long yoga class inspired by the Spring Equinox and the sense of balance, renewal, and new beginnings it brings. This grounding session invites you to reconnect with yourself as the seasons shift and will include breath awareness, gentle movement, and a guided meditation. Maggie is an E-RYT 200 certified yoga teacher with over eight years of teaching experience and specializes in beginner-friendly, accessible, alignment-based yoga. This class is well suited for those new to yoga as well as seasoned yogis looking for a mindful, nurturing practice.When she’s not on the mat, Maggie loves to paint, sculpt in clay, spend time in nature or her garden, and be with loved ones. She and her partner also regularly volunteer at Lancaster Farm Sanctuary in Mount Joy, PA. 



After the session, attendees are invited to shop and enjoy an exclusive 20% off before 12pm. 



Please note that this class will be held upstairs in our Loft area and is accessible by stairs only.

title: Children's Chapel
location: Saint James Episcopal Church, 103 North Duke Street, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Children’s Chapel* meets weekly during the 10:30 AM Sunday Mass. Children and youth ages 3 and above begin the morning in the sanctuary where they can experience the processional and the opening congregational prayers. 
After the opening Collect, children are invited to the front of the sanctuary to make their way to the chapel and participate in songs, prayers, brief meditation and storytelling of the day’s Gospel and/or New and Old Testament readings. 
The Godly Play® teaching method includes using beautiful wooden figures, candles, and symbolic colors to aid our storytelling as we listen to God’s word for us. Children often respond to the story using simple art supplies including crayons, construction paper, origami paper and Model Magic air dry clay. 
Children return to the Sanctuary during the passing of the Peace. They are welcome to take part in Holy Communion.

title: Sunday Afternoon Ride the River Trail 24F RSVP Required
description:
Lancaster Bicycle Club (LBC) Meetup
join your friends of the Lancaster Bike Club and Rails and Trails Club for a Sunday afternoon ride on the Northwest River Trail. Plenty of street parking around 40 East Front Street, Marietta, across from the River Trail Pub. We leave at 1pm and ride from Marietta to the Falmouth Boat Launch. This trail is paved; any kind of bike will do. This is a casual ride. Please do not sign up if you will not stay with the group. No one will be left behind. 24 miles total flat along the mighty Susquehanna River. After returning to our cars, those who would like yummy food and drinks at the River trail Pub. Like always helmets are required and have a tube for your bike so someone can fix a flat.
To eliminate passing around a pen and paper waiver, we encourage LBC Club membership and Meetup RSVP below to join this ride. RSVP also allows us to contact you after the ride if needed. If you do not RSVP you are not covered by the club excess accident insurance policy; only club members who RSVP are covered.
Richard Conner, leader 717-572-8480

title: Mark Lowry with Endless Highway and The Sound - American Music Theatre
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Highway East, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Mark Lowry is known around the world as a trusted voice in the realm of gospel music and beyond.  He started making music at age 11 and now, more than five decades later, his legacy is forever sealed as an innately entertaining communicator who can, at once, make audiences laugh, cry, and think.  Mark is a singer, storyteller, humorist, author, and songwriter, whose lyric to “Mary, Did You Know?” resulted in one of the most loved modern Christmas songs of this century. 
Endless Highway is a rapidly rising family group with unmatchable harmonies and a stylistically diverse program: combining elements of acoustic and bluegrass music alongside sweeping orchestrations that effortlessly blend to create unforgettable performances.

A contemplative candlelit service to nurture mind, body and spirit 
Join us for Vespers, a warm and inviting weekly gathering in the serene surroundings of the Saint James Chapel. This quiet, reflective service embraces the rich prayer traditions of Christianity and the embodied practices that nurture our spiritual lives today. 
Engage in uplifting singing, find solace in moments of silence, and choose the posture that feels most comfortable for you as we open our hearts to listen for God’s voice. title: Being One Online Spiritual Support Group
description:
Being One Center in Lancaster County
Life can be frustrating and distressing!
Sometimes we just feel overwhelmed, insecure, or lost!

The good news is, you're not alone!

We warmly invite you to check-in and connect with community in our Online Spiritual Support Group. In each meeting, we will discuss our current stressors and see how we can positively cope using our spiritual tools to overcome these challenges.

Together we can help each other grow and expand our consciousness for these high vibrational times.

Donations Greatly Appreciated!

This Event is lovingly hosted By Anna Bojczuk. Anna is a Registered nurse with over 40 years of experience as well as a gifted Energy worker. Anna is also long-time volunteer and inspiration in the Being One Center Community.

Donations to the Being One Center are greatly appreciated
(https://www.paypal.com/donate?token=AzIxMnj72R-pxLPP0cGQILM7OPiZs5zbsOdxGA7LFH60Wkr_0YcLATVAkIHYVjrFl4PP36X2GkDEJbaM)

title: Evening Compline
location: Saint James Episcopal Church, 103 North Duke Street, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Evening Compline
via Zoom
Click here to join Compline. (This link can be used for every evening Compline on Zoom.) 

The short service of Compline is based on the closing time of prayer in the Daily Office, or monastic cycle of prayer. It is observed in monasteries around 8 or 9 PM when darkness has fallen and bedtime is near. 

The thoughts that inspire the prayers during Compline are the need for divine forgiveness and protection against the powers of darkness. Prayers for peace and rest are offered as night falls. 

People of all faiths and beliefs are invited to this contemplative service. The service lasts about 20 minutes.

title: St. Patrick's Day at Annie Bailey's Irish Public House
location: Annie Bailey’s Irish Pub & Restaurant, 28-30 East King Street, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
St. Patrick’s Day at Annie Bailey’s isn’t just a day—it’s a five-day celebration running March 13th through March 17th. As one of the only true Irish pubs in town, this is what we do best. We’ll be pouring all your Irish favorites—Guinness, Harp Lager, Smithwick’s, and Magners—alongside a special food menu packed with hearty Irish staples. 



Expect late-night entertainment all weekend long, plus a special appearance by Irish dancers bringing authentic energy and tradition to the pub. On St. Patrick’s Day itself, we’re opening early so you can celebrate from the first pint to the last call. Gather your crew, wear your green, and stop in to experience St. Patrick’s the way it’s meant to be—loud, lively, and unmistakably Irish. 



Sláinte! 🍻

title: Ignatian Spirituality
location: Saint James Parish House Library
description:
Ignatian Spirituality
Tuesday evenings from 5:15 – 6:15 PM
September 9 – April 28, 2026
Ignatian spirituality is a way of living spirituality in everyday life. It asserts that God is present in our world and actively involved in our lives. It is a pathway to deeper prayer, good decisions guided by keen discernment and an active life of service to others. 
This 34-week program is designed for individuals who wish to commit to personal transformation by encountering God and deepening their experience of the divine. It will involve a daily and weekly commitment to spiritual practices with help and support from spiritual director Pat Latshaw. 
The book, The Ignatian Adventure by Kevin O’Brien will be used for discussion and to guide the group’s approach to spiritual growth with techniques that have been in use for almost 500 years. 
The cost is $875 for 34 sessions. Scholarships are available. 
Have questions about scholarships and/or Ignatian Spirituality? Click here to email Pat Latshaw. 
Click here to register for the course.

title: Lancaster Scrapbooking & Papercrafting Monthly gathering
description:
Lancaster Scrapbooking, Cards & Papercrafts
Scrapbook, make cards, practice quilling, or paint!
Meeting on the third Tuesday, 6-10 p.m., at the Cozy Crop House. (Also meeting the next night, Wednesday, same time & place. You can come to one or both, and can leave your gear overnight.) A chance to meet & gather monthly with local scrapbookers & crafters, in an ideal crafting environment! Plenty of room to do your own thing while we socialize. Bring your own supplies & projects, or just sit back and relax with scrapping friends, and watch & learn a new technique.
Specific crafting events (eg. card classes, paint nights, etc) may be featured here as scheduled.
Bring snacks to share if you wish.
Meetup groups are charged a monthly administration fee which is currently paid in this group by a few members. VOLUNTARY donations of $1 or $2 into a group "kitty" will help us continue the group's open membership and online advertising. The rate it costs our _whole group_ is about $12/month for a 6-month commitment. Please do not feel you cannot come if you don't contribute--the fee is paid, and donations are voluntary to cover future fees.

Even dates: CC Tuesday Fitness Ride Even Days (26R)

title: Red Rose Blue Star Mom's Chapter Meeting
Contemplative Tuesday
location: Saint James Chapel, 103 North Duke Street, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
7 – 8:00 PM in the Chapel
Tuesdays, February 10 – March 24
In person and via Zoom 
This seven-week series explores the question: What kind of person must I become to live non-violently? 
Together, we will reflect on what we mean by non-violence—and what we mean by violence—drawing from scripture, contemplative practice, and the wisdom of those throughout history who have wrestled with this question before us. Through guided reflection, shared conversation and embodied practices, we will explore how inner formation shapes outer action, and how a grounded, compassionate presence becomes the foundation for loving resistance in the world. 
Click here to register.

title: Evening Prayer
location: Zoom Video Conferencing, PA, United States
description:
Wednesday Evening Prayer
Wednesdays with the Saints
Click here  to join Wednesday Evening Prayer. (This link can be used for every Wednesday Evening Prayer on Zoom. Passcode=WedEP) 

Yours is the day, O God, yours also the night; you established the moon and the sun. You fixed all the boundaries of the earth; you made both summer and winter. Psalm 74:15,16 

I will bless the Lord who gives me counsel; my heart teaches me, night after night. I have set the Lord always before me; because he is at my right hand, I shall not fall. Psalm 16:7,8 

Daily prayers are said on a regular schedule at Saint James. On Wednesday evenings, we invite you to explore and celebrate the inspiring lives of the saints at our Evening Prayer service. Join us each week as we remember some of the people who shaped the Church through the centuries using readings, scripture, prayers and music! We use liturgy from Common Worship: Daily Prayer and the commemorations are from Lesser Feasts and Fasts, 2018. Some light incense is used on occasion. Invite a friend!

title: Trivia Night at Southern Market - Meet People Lancaster team
description:
Meet People Lancaster
Do you love trivia nights, but want to avoid the hassle of trying to wrangle up enough friends to field a team? Come play for a Meet People Lancaster team and make some new friends along the way!
Cost: FREE
Bar & food stations are all walk up. You’ll be on your own tab for the night.
Host: Mark Aumen
Please try to get there by 6:30PM, so we can get situated at a couple tables. Trivia starts at 7PM. If you come up early you’ll have time to mingle and get some food before trivia starts.
Location: 100 S Queen St, Lancaster, PA 17603
How to find the group: We have a couple acrylic sign holders on tables with Meet People Lancaster logos in them. We’ll also post an Instagram story with a picture of where your host, Mark, is sitting in the food court. Please follow Instagram stories for event updates. Not just for this event, but for all events.

Central PA Game Club
The Dillsburg Game Night has been going strong since 2016. We meet every Wednesday from 6:30-9:30pm for casual tabletop games.
We are a group of about 30-40 gamers who love to learn and teach new games. You won’t find a more welcoming group… it’s not that we’re desperate for you to come back or anything like that 😉. But in all seriousness, it’s a great group of friendly people.
Given the timeframe we typically just do light to medium weight Euros. We have 200+ board games available to play and people typically bring games.
We run long game days every other month (usually a Saturday 2pm-9pm). We do Magic the Gathering on the first Wednesday of ever month (in addition to boardgames). We do monthly themed nights. Check out our Facebook for additional events.
Cost: Free
Who: All ages (under 15 must be accompanied by an adult)
When: Every Wednesday (6:30pm-9:30pm)
Where: Calvary UMC of Dillsburg, in the Family Center
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/2249345335100181
Thanks!

title: Louder than Guns
location: The Ware Center – Millersville University, 42 N. Prince Street, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description: Join us for a free screening of Louder than Guns, a music film about gun violence and gun rights in America, plus a community discussion and Q&A. Old Crow Medicine Show’s Ketch Secor and NPR’s David Greene journey through rural, urban, and suburban America, having a conversation that humanizes all sides of this country’s polarized gun debate. Through the convening power of music and civil discourse, Secor and Greene help those they meet to find unexpected common ground. Come early for a pre-show community discussion panel at 6:15 pm – then stay after the film for a post-screening Q&A with David Greene. Presented in partnership with the Steinman Institute for Civic Engagement.

title: Evening Compline
location: Saint James Episcopal Church, 103 North Duke Street, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Evening Compline
via Zoom
Click here to join Compline. (This link can be used for every evening Compline on Zoom.) 

The short service of Compline is based on the closing time of prayer in the Daily Office, or monastic cycle of prayer. It is observed in monasteries around 8 or 9 PM when darkness has fallen and bedtime is near. 

The thoughts that inspire the prayers during Compline are the need for divine forgiveness and protection against the powers of darkness. Prayers for peace and rest are offered as night falls. 

People of all faiths and beliefs are invited to this contemplative service. The service lasts about 20 minutes.

Five years after their chance encounter in a department store, we set sail with our beloved ladies for more high jinks on the high seas. Menopause The Musical 2: Cruising Through ‘The Change’® is a hilarious and heartfelt look at the joys of menopause and friendship — plus hot flashes, mood swings, and memory lapses! Join us on a trip of self-discovery, backed by a new soundtrack of toe-tapping parodied hits from the 70s, 80s and 90s. title: The Varied Faces of James Buchanan
location: LancasterHistory, 230 North President Avenue, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
Following 21 years as Director of President James Buchanan’s Wheatland, Patrick Clarke has decided to retire. Early in his tenure as Director of James Bcuhanan’s Wheatland, Clarke discovered that most visitors, like himself, knew very little about James Buchanan’s early career in Pennsylvania and national politics, and they knew even less about his private life. Driven by his curiosity, Clarke decided to delve deep into primary research on Buchanan’s childhood and education, as well as the lives of his parents, siblings, nieces, and nephews, and the lives of the individuals who worked as domestic servants at his farm. 



On Thursday March 19, Clarke joins LancasterHistory once more to share how and why he reshaped the interpretative approach at James Buchanan’s Wheatland. He will share the fascinating, varied faces of the man he has come to know as Buchanan the politician and campaigner, the family patriarch, father and disciplinarian, and the Sage of Wheatland.

title: Art Battle Lancaster - March Event
location: West Art Community Center, 816 Buchanan Ave, Lancaster, Pennsylvania, 17603, United States
description:
Art Battle Lancaster is a live, high-energy painting competition where artists create original works in front of an audience. During each round, selected artists have just 20 minutes to transform a blank canvas into a finished painting while guests watch the creative process unfold in real time. 



At the end of each round, the audience votes for their favorite artist to advance to the final round, where the night’s champion is crowned. The fast-paced format makes every round exciting, as creativity, speed, and style all play a role in winning over the crowd. 



All of the paintings created during the competition will be available in a silent auction, giving attendees the opportunity to take home one-of-a-kind artwork created during the event. 



In addition to the tournament rounds, the evening will feature a special guest artist painting live throughout the event. That finished piece will also be auctioned at the end of the night. 



Art Battle Lancaster brings together artists, art lovers, and the local community for a fun and energetic night celebrating creativity. Whether you are cheering on your favorite painter, discovering new artists, or hoping to take home a piece of original artwork, every Art Battle event offers a unique experience.

description:
Be part of our March Mocktails & Meaning™ gathering as we welcome Dr. Oliver Dreon, Professor of Educational Foundations at Millersville University, for a timely conversation on Artificial Intelligence in Education. With more than thirty years in public education and experience teaching at the middle school, high school, undergraduate, and graduate levels, Dr. Dreon brings a broad and thoughtful perspective on how technology is reshaping classrooms and learning. Together, we’ll explore the opportunities, challenges, and big questions AI is raising for students and educators alike. We’ll be sipping a refreshing Pineapple and Cucumber Juice mocktail while we engage this important topic, with time set aside for questions and discussion.

We are excited to welcome this worldwide phenomenon back to American Music Theatre! Since Riverdance first emerged onto the world stage, its fusion of Irish and international dance and music has captured the hearts of millions worldwide. The Grammy award-winning music and the infectious energy of its mesmerizing choreography and breathtaking performances has left audiences in awe and established Riverdance as a global cultural sensation. 
This spectacular production rejuvenates the much-loved original show with new innovative choreography and costumes and state of the art lighting, projection and motion graphics. And for the first time Riverdance welcomes “The New Generation” of performers, all of whom were not born when the show began 30 years ago. 
Audiences will enjoy a unique and memorable performance which blends the traditional and the contemporary, showcasing the skill and passion of the world-class dancers, musicians and singers in the Riverdance ensemble.

title: Dr. Seuss The Cat in the Hat – Live on Stage! at American Music Theatre
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Highway East, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Here’s an idea: Bring the whole family to the Worlds Number 1 ‘The Cat in the Hat’ show for an experience they’ll never forget. The story has been around for over 50 years and kids still love it… just like you did! 
Two bored kids (This was way before iPads). One rainy day (We’ve all had a few of those) And one crazy cat wearing a red-striped hat (Where was the background check for that babysitter?!) 
In 1957, Dr Seuss used only 200 words to write his classic tale. More than 50 years later, The Cat in the Hat is a much-loved children’s phenomenon. The play was adapted for the stage in 2009 by the National Theatre of Britain. The Cat in The Hat interrupts this rainy afternoon with his unexpected visit to Sally and her brother’s cozy home. Their outspoken and outraged pet Fish is astounded and concerned, but this cat will not be deterred. He will teach us all to make our own fun with nothing but a little imagination. “It’s fun to have fun, but you have to know how” Along with Thing One and Thing Two, the cat will fill the theatre with mischievous humour and madcap style. 
A timeless tale with a twist—this energetic adaptation bursts onto the stage (and maybe into the aisles). Expect laughs, surprises, and just a bit of delightful disorder!

title: Spring Greening the Lower Susquehanna - Riparian Buffer Maintenance
location: Overlook Park, 595 Granite Run Dr., Lancaster, PA, 17601, United States
description: Join Stroud Water Research Center as they manage a large tree planting at Overlook Park. They need help weeding, straightening tubes, replanting, removing tubes, and other tasks that are really important for helping the trees to grow and establish. There is a pollinator planting on site, too. Email Lamonte at lgarber@stroudcenter.org with the subject line “Volunteer at Overlook Park” to get all the details.

title: Brain Discovery Day: Presented by Penn State Health
location: The Lancaster Science Factory, 454 New Holland Ave, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Explore Your Amazing Mind
We’ve got the brain on the brain! Join us on Saturday, March 21st, from 10 am – 3 pm as we host Brain Discovery Day! All activities will be presented FREE with admission! 
Enjoy 80+ hands-on exhibits PLUS:
Expose Your Nose: Take the interactive smell challenge at the Science Cafe to learn how your brain interprets scent and why smells are strongly connected to memory and emotion. 
Brain Dissection Demonstrations: The Millersville University Women in STEM will host live sheep brain dissections. Observe and identify parts of the brain and learn how our brains are different- and surprisingly similar! 
Stroke Detectives: The Penn State Milton S. Hershey Medical Center will facilitate this interactive program that turns children into rookie detectives, teaching them about and identifying the basics of stroke and common risk factors. Solve the mystery and help decode our amazing brains! 
Click here to purchase advance tickets for Saturday, March 21st. Tickets will also be sold at the door. 
Members and those using free-admission programs should not purchase tickets in advance.

title: Spring Ephemerals of the Susquehanna River Hills
location: LancasterHistory, 230 North President Avenue, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description: Trilliums, wild columbine, Dutchman’s Breeches. These are a few of the delicate spring ephemerals, early season wildflowers, that are the stars of woodlands and meadows for a short window of time. Peggy Eppig, Conservation Education Ranger for the Lancaster Conservancy, will connect the dots between ephemerals, the pollinators who rely on them, the landscapes in which they thrive, and where you can find them in the man Lancaster Conservancy preserves throughout the lower Susquehanna Valley.

—————————————————
Presented by Mickey’s Black Box
—————————————————
Previously billed as “Southern Rock Night” 
Join Remedy, Hotlanta, and Rocks This Way for a night celebrating – you guessed it – Black Crowes, The Allman Brothers & Aerosmith – playing your favorite classic rock tunes! 
Remedy
Remedy is a tribute to The Black Crowes , a southern hard blues-rock jam band. They play The Black Crowes’ tops hits, fan favorites, and deep tracks. Remedy performs a high-energy rock n roll set which has earned them a reputation as one of the tri-state’s top tribute acts!  
Hotlanta
Capturing the creative spirit of the music created by one of the most prolific rock bands in American music history is not something one decides to do without inspiration. In fact, it is inspiration of the soul that has brought together the members of HOTLANTA.  
The grind of the every-day, the mundane if you will, is what causes this manifestation of energy and the desire to grab hold of music so special; soul music, bluesy, country, folk, funk, down-home gospel inspired music of one of the worlds greatest jam bands ever. “The” jam band it could be said. Experience the emergence of an inspired group of people joining together in a single, common cause. That being to recreate a feeling, a vibe, a spirit of music and love and passion. The music of an American icon, the music of The Allman Brothers Band. 
Rocks This Way
Rocks This Way is a hard-hitting Aerosmith tribute that channels the grit, swagger, and soul of the Bad Boys from Boston. With explosive guitar work, soaring vocals, and a stadium-ready energy, they bring the full Aerosmith experience to life. A perfect blend of deep cuts, mega-hits, and authentic attitude makes every show feel like the real thing.

title: Play It Forward: A Benefit Show for Lancaster Neighbor Fund
location: Lancaster Improv Players, 16 S Prince St, Lancaster, Pennsylvania, 17603, United States
description:
Using improv to build a better world; one ticket at a time! Proceeds from ticket sales will go to the Lancaster Neighbor Fund. 



Since 2015, Lancaster Improv Players has served our city through laughter; above all, our mission is to make Lancaster a funnier place. Humor can be a balm in trying times, and we wouldn’t be the first to say that laughter can be healing. Another important component of building a better world is making sure the programs helping out people in trying times are properly funded, which is why we are going to PLAY IT FORWARD! 



All proceeds from this event’s ticket sales will benefit the Lancaster Neighbor Fund. For those unfamiliar with LNF, they provide direct financial support to immigrants who are vulnerable to deportation from Lancaster County. You can find out more about them and incredible work they do at their website; https://lancasterneighborfund.org/ 



Enjoy an hour of uproarious improv comedy from a curated collection of local talent, AND build a better world through community care. It’s time to Play It Forward! 



FEATURING: GLORZEBUB and HIPPIE & FRENCHIE! 



BYOB$15 at the door 



(Our tickets are on a sliding scale; you are free to pay at a higher price point to boost the benefit!) 



Due to the size of our theater, there is a quick turnover time between our scheduled shows. If you purchase tickets to both events on Saturday evening, you will be asked to wait outside during the break so that the next team can warm up before the audience returns.

title: Asking the Hard Questions
location: Church of the Apostles UCC, 1850 Marietta Ave, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
At Church of the Apostles, we know many are carrying deep and honest questions about faith, especially in light of what is happening in our country and throughout the world. Questions like “Where is God in all of this?” are not signs of weak faith, but part of a faithful journey we are called to walk together. 
To create space for these conversations, we invite you to a new monthly series, Asking the Hard Questions, hosted by our seminary intern, Deklan Lewis, in the Parlor. Together, we will explore doubt, struggle, and belief as meaningful parts of discipleship. Our first two gatherings will include viewing and discussing the film God on Trial as we wrestle, much like Jacob, with faith and what it means to follow Jesus today. 
To accommodate different schedules, two sessions will be offered each month: Monday morning, March 23rd at 10 AM, and Thursday evening, March 26th at 6 PM. All are welcome to join this thoughtful, open, and supportive conversation.

title: Democratic Faith: From Abolitionism to Civil Rights
location: The Barshinger Center for Musical Arts, College Avenue, Lancaster, PA
description:
A moral and political vision of democratic faith has guided the struggle for racial justice from the abolitionist movement in the 19th century to the civil rights movement in the 20th century. Figures such as Thaddeus Stevens and Lydia Hamilton Smith, alongside David Walker, Frederick Douglass, Anna Julia Cooper, and Martin Luther King Jr., reveal that faith in democracy is not a matter of optimism but of moral courage—the conviction that equality must be enacted in both public institutions and everyday life. Drawing on this inheritance, Dr. Melvin L. Rogers of Brown University, considers how such faith can sustain us amid the moral and political crises of our own time. In the year of America’s Semiquincentennial, these questions lie at the heart of our past, present, and future.

 

Dr. Melvin L. Rogers is the Edna and Richard Salomon Distinguished Professor of Political Science and Co-Director of the Democracy Project at Brown University. A leading scholar of American and African American political thought, his work examines the moral and spiritual foundations of democracy. He is the author of the award-winning The Darkened Light of Faith: Race, Democracy, and Freedom in African American Political Thought (Princeton University Press, 2023), The Undiscovered Dewey: Religion, Morality, and the Ethos of Democracy (University of Chicago Press, 2009), and co-editor of African American Political Thought: A Collected History (University of Chicago Press, 2021).

 

Information & Registration

The lecture, “Democratic Faith: From Abolitionism to Civil Rights,” will take place on Thursday, March 24, 2026 at 7:30pm at the Ann & Richard Barshinger Center for Musical Arts at Franklin & Marshall College, located along College Avenue near the intersection with W. Frederick Street in Lancaster. [Click here for a campus map.] Parking is available on-street or in nearby Franklin & Marshall lots., or in nearby Franklin & Marshall lots. [Click here for a map of campus parking.]

 

This lecture is free and open to the public, but registration is required to guarantee a seat. At this time, in-person attendance is the only option available. We plan to make a livestream option available. Please check back in early March for this option.

 

Register to Attend In Person

 

Event Update, 1/21/2026: Previous versions of this event listing included a start time of 7pm. Due to changes with the venue, the start time of this program is anticipated to be 7:30pm. This change and any future changes will be communicated to ticketholders. Thank you!

 
This event is presented in partnership with Franklin & Marshall College with support from the Center for Politics and Public Affairs and Reckoning with Lancaster.

title: Dr. Seuss The Cat in the Hat - Live on Stage!
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $29 $39 $49 $59 
The kids are bored.. again..! What to do with YOUR Thing One and Thing 2?! 
Here’s an idea: Bring the whole family to the Worlds Number 1 ‘The Cat in the Hat’ show for an experience they’ll never forget. The story has been around for over 50 years and kids still love it… just like you did! 
Two bored kids (This was way before iPads). One rainy day (We’ve all had a few of those) And one crazy cat wearing a red-striped hat (Where was the background check for that babysitter?!) 
In 1957, Dr Seuss used only 200 words to write his classic tale. More than 50 years later, The Cat in the Hat is a much-loved children’s phenomenon. The play was adapted for the stage in 2009 by the National Theatre of Britain. The Cat in The Hat interrupts this rainy afternoon with his unexpected visit to Sally and her brother’s cozy home. Their outspoken and outraged pet Fish is astounded and concerned, but this cat will not be deterred. He will teach us all to make our own fun with nothing but a little imagination. “It’s fun to have fun, but you have to know how” Along with Thing One and Thing Two, the cat will fill the theatre with mischievous humour and madcap style. 
A timeless tale with a twist—this energetic adaptation bursts onto the stage (and maybe into the aisles). Expect laughs, surprises, and just a bit of delightful disorder! 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

Exploring the science of sound as I scrap together some studio equipment with little more than some magnets and a stick of gum.

**Tyler Golden - Fun with Sensors: Tracking Beers Poured on St. Patrick's Day**
Tyler will present his latest iteration of a yearly hobby project - tracking drafts poured at the local pub on St. Patrick's Day. Using inexpensive sensors and microcontroller boards, he’ll demonstrate how to measure the amounts being poured, stream the data in real time, and visualize the flow on a public dashboard.

7:45 PM Speaker 2

8:15 PM End

title: The Indie Journalist Meetup
description:
The Creative House of Lancaster
Join us for the inaugural gathering of the Indie Journalist Meetup at [The Candy Factory](www.coworkinginlancaster.com) (TCF), 342 N. Queen St., Lancaster.

We’re looking to gather digital journalists from the greater Lancaster area who are navigating the creator economy to produce content covering news, events, and lifestyle/cultural features. This can include podcasters, bloggers, writers, livestreamers, and more.

In this first meetup, our goal is to take inventory of what’s happening in our slice of the information ecosystem here in Lancaster. Let’s share the projects we’re working on and what we hope to accomplish with them. We will also brainstorm ideas for what participants might find valuable as the meetup develops.

**ABOUT THE INDIE JOURNALIST MEETUP**
A meetup for independent journalists covering news, events, culture, and lifestyle while navigating the creator economy in Lancaster and surrounding areas. All creators welcome: podcasters, freelance writers, bloggers, YouTubers, livestreamers, and more!

Our mission is to create a space to gather in real life, to discuss the rewards and challenges of bringing authentic news and information to the Lancaster community, and to encourage participants to collaborate and share knowledge when appropriate to strengthen our local information ecosystem.

**ABOUT YOUR HOST**
Jason Mundok is a seasoned media producer with over two decades of experience creating compelling content and leading creative teams. At So Good Media, he produces audio podcasts, marketing videos, and original programming.

He currently hosts *[So Good Lancaster](https://sogoodlancaster.com/)*, a show that celebrates the people and ideas that make Lancaster, PA, so good.

The Creative House of Lancaster is a project of [The Candy Factory, a coworking + social club](http://www.coworkinginlancaster.com/) located in the heart of Lancaster, PA.

description:
2026 Gap Fire Company Sale
Our 50th Annual fire company “mud sale” consignment auction is being held on Friday evening March 27th and Saturday March 28th. 
As in past years, it will be held at 836 Houston Run Drive, Gap on the grounds of Lancaster County Showcase. Please enter from the Route 30 and Newport Rd entrance (across from the Rutter’s convenience store/gas station). There will be limited on-site parking within a 1/4 mile, while the remainder of the parking will be a 1/2 mile away with a shuttle school bus to bring you to the sale grounds. There will also be limited handicap parking spaces with a placard. 
The receiving dates and times to bring your consigned or donated items are as follows: Wed. 3/25: 10 AM to 7 PM, Thurs. 3/26: 8 AM to 7 PM, Friday 3/27: 8 AM to 3:00 PM. All items to be consigned are to be brought to the sale location at 836 Houston Run Drive. 
Notice: No poultry equipment, tires, hazardous equipment, TVs, computers, used appliances, used sofas, box lots, barrels or broken furniture. Gap Fire Company and the receiving attendants reserve the right to reject or accept any items. 
A Variety of food & drinks will be available throughout the day for all to enjoy! 

If you can’t make it this weekend, check out or full list of upcoming Mud Sales on LancasterPA.com.

title: Mitchell Tenpenny: Speed of Light Tour 2026 with special guest Jake Banfield
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $49 $59 $69 $89 
Multi-platinum-selling artist Mitchell Tenpenny has seen his career explode over the last few years. Since the release of his debut #1 hit single “Drunk Me” the multi-faceted entertainer, singer, songwriter, producer and performer has surpassed the One Billion streams threshold, now at more than 2.6 Billion global streams and growing. His RIAA certifications continue to soar with recent recognitions for “Drunk Me” hitting 4-times Platinum and “We Got History” garnering Platinum status. 
Mitchell’s additional #1 singles include “Truth About You” and “At The End Of A Bar” with Chris Young. With the latter (at the time of its chart peak), Mitchell made history with shortest span between #1 songs since 1982 with just a three-week stretch. 
From the Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon to the Today Show and numerous other television appearances, Mitchell has performed his music to an ever-growing legion of fans. His success as a touring artist has grown from recent guest slots on the road with Jason Aldean, Luke Combs and Jordan Davis, to his own headline tours. 
Rising singer-songwriter Jake Banfield delivers heartfelt lyrics, rich vocals, and a fresh blend of folk, country, and pop. With an engaging stage presence and emotionally resonant songs about life, love, and hometown roots, his live performances feel both intimate and energizing. 
Discover a rising artist whose authentic sound and genuine connection leave a lasting impression. It’s the perfect night out for fans of modern Americana and contemporary singer-songwriters.

title: Make & Take: Stained Glass Dragonfly
location: Bird-in-Hand Artisan Village, 2705 Old Philadelphia Pike, Bird-in-Hand, PA, 17505, United States
description:
An antique key becomes a thing of beauty to hang or wear 
Welcome spring! Create a colorful dragonfly with stained glass wings and finish it as a necklace or a sun catcher to capture the rays streaming in through the windows. Learn how under the guidance of local artisan Maria Roshon, of Fractured Frog. Whether she is making jewelry, suncatchers or custom windows or transoms, she unites color and light with gorgeous results. 
In this workshop, you’ll choose from a variety of glass colors, and then Maria will lead you through the process of turning them into wings and soldering them to a genuine antique key, then finishing them off with a black cord for use as a necklace or a ribbon for hanging. Choose from four convenient start times and go home with a beautiful, one-of-a-kind creation. Reservations are recommended; limited space for walk-ins may be available. 
$45 includes instruction and all supplies. 
Date: Saturday, March 28
Times: 10 a.m., 11:30 a.m., 1 p.m. and 2:30 p.m. 
To save your space: E-mail us or call (717) 344-6875. Reservations are strongly recommended as class size is limited.

title: Solar Federation: A Tribute to Rush
location: Mickey’s Black Box, 101 Rock Lititz Boulevard, Lititz, PA, 17543, United States
description:
Saturday March 28th, 2026
Doors | 7:00 PM
Show | 8:00 PM 
General Admission Standing Room | $36.75 + Fees
Reserved Balcony & Mezzanine Seating | $38.80 + Fees 
+$5 on All Day of Show Purchases
This event is all ages – 21+ With Valid ID to Drink
Come hungry! Snacks and light bites will be available at the canteen.
—————————————————
Presented by Mickey’s Black Box
—————————————————
Solar Federation:
Mike Bitts – bass, keyboards
Ffej Herb – drums, cymbals, percussion
Julie Schreiber – lead vocals
Eric Wirsing – guitar, backing vocals 
Hailing from Central Pennsylvania, Solar Federation performs the music of Rush with passion and precision, tuning into all the fine details die-hard fans analyze with every spin of an album. Formed in 2014, the band spent an entire year honing its initial set list and rehearsing what is often called the most complex and difficult catalog of songs to perform in the pantheon of rock music. After a handful of live performances, the band quickly established itself as one of the premier Rush tribute bands on the East Coast, garnering praise from fans for its accurate interpretation of the material, meticulous attention to detail, and vocalist Julie Schreiber’s passionate delivery of Rush’s dynamic melodies and thought-provoking lyrics. Over the last nine years, Solar Federation has become known as a faithful tribute to the music of Rush and has garnered respect among the vast community of Rush fans around the globe. 
Following the death of Peart, Solar Federation was invited to perform at A Night for Neil, a memorial tribute concert organized by Peart’s parents and family as well as the nonprofit organization, Overtime Angels. The concert was held in Peart’s hometown of St. Catharines, Ontario, at the Meridian Center on Saturday, October 22, 2022. Solar Federation was the only Rush tribute band from the United States invited to perform. The band headlined the event with an hour-long closing set that brought down the house of more than 4,000 fans. 
In late 2023, Solar Federation had the tremendous honor of collaborating with Rush’s longtime album cover artist, Hugh Syme, and legendary record producer, Terry Brown, on a studio version of “Witch Hunt” from Rush’s 1981 masterpiece, Moving Pictures. The track premiered on April 12, 2024, on 97.3 WRVV The River and is available on all major streaming platforms. It will also appear on the forthcoming Songs for Neil: Volume 5, set to release internationally on September 12, 2024. The band’s live versions of “The Necromancer” and “Chemistry” have appeared on the previous Songs for Neil tribute albums, with proceeds of sales benefitting Cancer Support Scotland (UK) and Glioblastoma Foundation Neil Peart research Award (USA). 
Solar Federation was voted “Best Tribute Band” at the 2023 Central PA Music Awards, an accolade it also received in 2021 and for which it was nominated in 2022. 
“You can’t fake it with this band,” said Ffej Herb, Solar Federation’s drummer. “Rush fans are incredibly loyal and know this music inside and out.” 
Rush is widely recognized for its legions of devoted fans from all corners of the world. “I’m constantly floored to learn that fans from all over the Mid-Atlantic region travel to attend our shows,” said Herb. “To me, that not only speaks volumes about the loyalty of Rush fans, but it’s also a testament to the quality of our little band.” 
Check out Solar Federation here!

title: LFS Monthly Discussion - El Serrano's
description:
Lancaster Freethought Society
Come joins us for intelligent discussion and freethinking fun at our monthly meeting. Lancaster Freethought Society is a community for Freethinkers, Atheists, Agnostics, Humanists, Non-believers, Skeptics, and those who are simply curious. There is no fee to attend our meetings which range from topical subjects of interest to freethinkers such as Church/State Separation, as well as subjects such as science, philosophy, ethics, counter-apologetics, and psychology. All are welcome to attend no matter what your beliefs may be but proselytizing is not allowed. Bring your articles, news, jokes, gripes, questions, ideas, topics, or anything else you would like to discuss with the rest of the group.

Hope to see you there!

title: The Simon & Garfunkel Story
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $39 $49 $59 $69 
VIP 1&2: The Simon & Garfunkel Story VIP Experience  $172-$182 
The Simon & Garfunkel Story VIP Experience Includes: 

One Ticket with in the first 10 rows
A private 20-minute acoustic performance with the lead performers of The Simon & Garfunkel Story for VIP attendees only, featuring exclusive Simon & Garfunkel songs not performed in the main show. “Stripped back, with one guitar and two voices… singing in harmony.”
A Meet & Greet and photo opportunity with the two lead performers of The Simon & Garfunkel Story.
A limited edition, collectible VIP laminate.

VIP UPGRADE: The Simon & Garfunkel Story VIP Experience Upgrade $113  
The Simon & Garfunkel Story VIP Experience Upgrade* Includes: 

A private 20-minute acoustic performance with the lead performers of The Simon & Garfunkel Story for VIP attendees only, featuring exclusive Simon & Garfunkel songs not performed in the main show. “Stripped back, with one guitar and two voices… singing in harmony.”
A Meet & Greet and photo opportunity with the two lead performers of The Simon & Garfunkel Story.
A limited edition, collectible VIP laminate.

*Upgrade packages DO NOT INCLUDE TICKETS. A ticket is required to enter the venue. 
Now seen in 50 countries worldwide, The Simon and Garfunkel Story is a critically acclaimed concert style theatre show about two young boys from Queens, New York who went on to become the world’s most successful music duo of all time.  Using state of the art video projection, incredible lighting, and a full live band, The Simon & Garfunkel Story is a moving and powerful concert featuring all the hits such as “Mrs. Robinson,” “Bridge Over Troubled Water,” “Homeward Bound,” “Scarborough Fair,” “The Boxer,” “The Sound Of Silence,” and many more. 
For certain concerts, audience members may prefer to stand or dance, especially when encouraged by the artist to do so. Guest expectations differ, so we simply ask all attendees to extend mutual respect toward one another and maintain awareness of those around them. Our staff will only intervene in matters of safety or when behavior is deemed unruly, combative, or disruptive to the overall performance.

title: The Temptations & The Four Tops
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $79 $89 $99 $109
AMT Member Pre-Sale: Wednesday, August 13 at 10:00 a.m.
Public On-Sale: Friday, August 15 at 10:00 a.m. 
Often referred to as American Music Royalty, The Temptations are world-renowned superstars of entertainment, revered for their phenomenal catalog of music and prolific career.  Thanks to their fine-tuned choreography, and even finer harmonies, The Temptations became one of the most successful acts to record for Motown Records.  During the group’s six-decade career, these Grammy winners have charted numerous #1 hits including “My Girl,” “Ain’t Too Proud to Beg,” “Get Ready,” “Just My Imagination,” and “Papa Was a Rollin’ Stone,” among many others. 
The Four Tops teamed up in high school and spent over four decades without a single personnel change to become one of Motown’s most consistent hit makers.  “Baby I Need Your Loving,” “I Can’t Help Myself (Sugar Pie, Honey Bunch),” “Reach Out (I’ll Be There),” “Standing in the Shadows of Love,” and “Ain’t No Woman (Like the One I’ve Got)” are just some of their classic hits.

title: Annual Garage Sale Day 1
location: Church of the Apostles UCC, 1850 Marietta Ave, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
Searching for a massive, budget-friendly yard sale in or near Lancaster? Look no further!
Join us at Church of the Apostles UCC for our annual garage sale held on the Friday and Saturday following Easter. Be prepared to shop early, as there’s always a long line before doors open on Friday! 
This years sale is set for April 10th from 8 AM – 2 PM & April 11th from 8 AM – 1 PM in and around Fellowship Hall. Our address is 1850 Marietta Avenue, Lancaster PA. Be sure to follow signs for Fellowship Hall! 
All are welcome to attend this fun-filled annual event. We have many low-priced items for you to browse through including clothing, collectables, home furnishings, jewelry, sports items, tools, toys and so much more! Food options include hotdogs, sodas and baked goods on Friday and Saturday! Ample parking, including accessible parking, is available on both days. 
If you are interested in donating items, you can do so the week of the sale, April 6th, 7th, & 8th, from 9 AM – 9 PM, or April 9th, from 9 AM – 12 PM, by the Fellowship Hall doors. For additional information, please feel free to contact us at office@apostlesucc.org or by calling (717) 392-5718.

Celebrate the freshness of early spring with Mike Wetherhold in this lively, hands-on sourdough workshop. Known for his incredible loaves (and his equally famous dad jokes), Mike will guide you through the complete process of creating and maintaining your own sourdough starter, mastering fermentation, and baking loaves that are crisp on the outside and airy within. You will explore seasonal flavors and spring-inspired variations, learn to care for your starter at home, and leave confident to keep the baking going long after class ends. Expect plenty of Q&A time, tasting, and laughter along the way. 
Demonstration. BYOB. Ages 16 and up. Vegetarian. 

Classic Country Sourdough Loaf
Herbed Spring Sourdough with Chives and Lemon Zest
Tasting of Fresh Sourdough with Freshly Made Butter and Seasonal Jams

title: Video Game Day
location: The Lancaster Science Factory, 454 New Holland Ave, Lancaster, PA, 17602, United States
description:
Game on! 
We’re partnering with the Lancaster Rec Esports program for a day of video game design and play! Compete in popular games like Super Smash Brothers™ and Mario Kart™. Players can test their skills, cheer on friends, and experience the excitement of esports in a welcoming, supportive environment.

Visitors of all ages can also participate in our Play for Impact Video Game Design Workshops. Participants will design, develop, and test their own video game using Scratch, a block-based programming tool.

Stay tuned for more video game fun as the event gets closer!  This event is presented free with admission.  
Click here to purchase tickets in advance for April 11th. Tickets will also be sold at the door.
Why Video Games and Esports?
Esports is one of the fastest-growing career fields in the world, offering opportunities not only as players but also in areas like computer programming, digital art, broadcasting, marketing, and game development. Colleges and universities, including local institutions, now offer esports teams, scholarships, and academic pathways that turn a passion for gaming into meaningful careers.

title: An Evening of Alton Brown
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $64 $74 $84 
VIP: Alton Brown VIP Experience: $200 
Includes: 

One ticket for admission
A special VIP experience one hour before show time.
It will include a brief Q&A session in the Theatre.
A signed copy of Alton’s book.

*There will be no individual photos or autographs, however, photos will be allowed during the experience.  Video is strictly prohibited. 
*If you purchase a VIP Experience, AMT will notify you by email prior to the event to give you additional instructions. 
Although he’s keeping the details under wraps, Brown has said that with An Evening of Alton Brown he’s “stirring up some twists on his greatest hits,” and “spicing things up with new demonstrations, music, and slightly scaled-down mayhem.”  No matter what, An Evening of Alton Brown will present Brown as you’ve never seen him — unless, of course, you’ve been invited to his house. 
Best known for creating and hosting groundbreaking television such as Good Eats, Iron Chef America, Cutthroat Kitchen, and Food Network Star, Brown has also built one of the most successful culinary touring franchises ever, performing live in more than 300 cities for well over half a million fans. With An Evening of Alton Brown, he strips away the spectacle in favor of connection, spontaneity, and storytelling, delivering a show that is equal parts humor, science, food, and heart.

title: Beginner Birder Trip to Riverfront Park
location: Riverfront Park Pavilion, 551 Vinegar Ferry Road, Marietta, PA, 17547, United States
description:

Beginner Birder field trip at Riverfront Park and journey along the Northwest River Trail along the Susquehanna River. This trip is designed for individuals who would like to experience birding for the first time or would like to further their birding observation skills. title: Nitty Gritty Dirt Band - All the Good Times: The Farewell Tour - 60 Years of Dirt
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $39 $49 $59 $79
 
Nitty Gritty Dirt Band has been delighting audiences with their signature blend of country, folk, rock, and roots music. Widely regarded as one of the most influential bands in American roots history, their career spans multiple generations and includes timeless hits like “Mr. Bojangles,” “Fishin’ in the Dark,” “An American Dream,” and “House at Pooh Corner.” 
The All the Good Times Tour celebrates the band’s incredible legacy — from their groundbreaking Will the Circle Be Unbroken collaborations to their chart-topping radio favorites. With their trademark harmonies, masterful musicianship, and heartfelt storytelling, The Nitty Gritty Dirt Band continues to prove that great music only gets better with age. 
Joining NGDB is emerging country artist Meels with her soulful voice, heartfelt lyrics, and modern country flair. Blending authentic storytelling with infectious melodies, Meels is quickly capturing hearts and carving out her place in today’s country music scene. 
VIP1: The Nitty Gritty Front Row with Meet & Greet Experience   $305

Premium reserved ticket in the front row

Exclusive signed 8×10 photo
Commemorative VIP laminate

title: Being One Online Spiritual Support Group
description:
Being One Center in Lancaster County
Life can be frustrating and distressing!
Sometimes we just feel overwhelmed, insecure, or lost!

The good news is, you're not alone!

We warmly invite you to check-in and connect with community in our Online Spiritual Support Group. In each meeting, we will discuss our current stressors and see how we can positively cope using our spiritual tools to overcome these challenges.

Together we can help each other grow and expand our consciousness for these high vibrational times.

Donations Greatly Appreciated!

This Event is lovingly hosted By Anna Bojczuk. Anna is a Registered nurse with over 40 years of experience as well as a gifted Energy worker. Anna is also long-time volunteer and inspiration in the Being One Center Community.

Donations to the Being One Center are greatly appreciated

title: Mocktails & Meaning™: Mental Health in Young Men
location: Church of the Apostles UCC, 1850 Marietta Ave, Lancaster, PA, 17603, United States
description:
April’s Mocktails & Meaning™ invites you into an honest and important conversation with Ben Faro from the Samaritan Center as we focus on Mental Health in Young Men. In a world that often teaches boys to stay silent about their struggles, this session will explore the pressures young men face, the warning signs we should not ignore, and the ways families, churches, and communities can foster openness, resilience, and hope. We’ll gather over a festive Easter Punch mocktail while creating space for thoughtful dialogue and meaningful questions. Come ready for a conversation that encourages courage, compassion, and connection. 
Register Now By Clicking Here

title: Folsom Prison Experience - A Johnny Cash Music/Dramatic Production
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $24 $34 $44 
Prepare to step back in time and immerse yourself in the unforgettable atmosphere of the legendary 1968 Johnny Cash show at Folsom Prison, where the spirit of that historic day springs to life. 
As the doors swing open, you’ll find yourself instantly transformed into an inmate, ready to embark on a captivating firsthand experience. Marvel at the commanding presence of Johnny Cash himself, joined by the incomparable June Carter and the melodious harmonies of the Statler Brothers. Sense the palpable tension as the watchful warden and his guards maintain order within the prison walls. 
Leading you through this extraordinary journey is the ever-serious emcee, Hugh Cherry. Get ready to be transported, as you become an integral part of this immersive tribute, capturing the raw energy and soul that made the Folsom Prison performance truly remarkable. A memorable show for all ages.

title: Lancaster Scrapbooking & Papercrafting Monthly gathering
description:
Lancaster Scrapbooking, Cards & Papercrafts
Scrapbook, make cards, practice quilling, or paint!
Meeting on the third Tuesday, 6-10 p.m., at the Cozy Crop House. (Also meeting the next night, Wednesday, same time & place. You can come to one or both, and can leave your gear overnight.) A chance to meet & gather monthly with local scrapbookers & crafters, in an ideal crafting environment! Plenty of room to do your own thing while we socialize. Bring your own supplies & projects, or just sit back and relax with scrapping friends, and watch & learn a new technique.
Specific crafting events (eg. card classes, paint nights, etc) may be featured here as scheduled.
Bring snacks to share if you wish.
Meetup groups are charged a monthly administration fee which is currently paid in this group by a few members. VOLUNTARY donations of $1 or $2 into a group "kitty" will help us continue the group's open membership and online advertising. The rate it costs our _whole group_ is about $12/month for a 6-month commitment. Please do not feel you cannot come if you don't contribute--the fee is paid, and donations are voluntary to cover future fees.

title: Shenandoah
location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $39 $49 $59 $79
 
For more than 30 years, Grammy Award-winning country band Shenandoah has delivered heartfelt songs and rich harmonies that helped shape the sound of modern country music. Led by original frontman Marty Raybon, the group rose to fame with beloved hits like “Two Dozen Roses,” “Church on Cumberland Road,” “Next to You, Next to Me,” and “I Want to Be Loved Like That.” 
Known for their genuine lyrics and timeless sound, Shenandoah continues to captivate audiences with a mix of nostalgia and new energy. Experience an evening filled with classic favorites and the unmistakable charm of one of country music’s most enduring groups.

location: American Music Theatre, 2425 Lincoln Hwy East, Lancaster, PA, 17605, United States
description:
Tickets: $29 $34 $39 
Stayin’ Alive offers to their audiences the songs and sights of a full Bee Gees play list, singing blockbusters such as “Night Fever,” “Jive Talkin’,” “How Deep Is Your Love,” “You Should Be Dancing,” “Nights on Broadway,” and “Stayin’ Alive.” In addition, they perform softer poetic ballads such as “I Started a Joke,” “Massachusetts,” “Fanny Be Tender,” “Words,” and “To Love Somebody” among other great hits. Stayin’ Alive is the largest and most definitive production of its kind, offering big screen video clips, photos, and dazzling imagery. Stayin’ Alive is the quintessential tribute band to the Bee Gees, capturing the excitement of live performance and the tender subtleties of the human voice.
